name: Sicilian Branzino
tagline: Whole branzino baked with tomatoes, olives, and capers
highlight: Whole-fish baking is foolproof — no shellfish, all coast

hero.alt: Whole branzino baked with tomatoes and olives
hero.caption: branzino sicilian — whole-baked

healthFacts: Whole fish keeps moisture in better than fillets — bones do work.
Mediterranean baking with olive oil retains polyphenols.

steps.title: Stuff the cavities
steps.detail: Pat fish dry, salt inside and out. Stuff cavities with sliced lemon, garlic, and thyme.
steps.tip: Cavity perfumes the meat from inside out — don't skip the stuffing.

media.alt: Branzino stuffed with lemon and herbs
media.caption: stuff the cavities

steps.title: Build the bed
steps.detail: Scatter cherry tomatoes, olives, capers on a sheet pan. Drizzle olive oil. Lay fish on top. Drizzle more oil.
steps.tip: Tomatoes cook into a silky pan sauce by the end.

steps.title: Roast hot
steps.detail: Roast at 425°F for 18 min. Eyes turn opaque, skin crisps, fish flakes off the bone.
steps.tip: Don't open the oven early; whole fish needs trapped heat.

steps.title: Plate and squeeze
steps.detail: Lift fish onto a platter; spoon tomato-olive juices over. Squeeze fresh lemon. Serve whole at the table.
steps.tip: Lift the top fillet, then the spine, then the bottom fillet — easier than it sounds.
